text_text: (1) Functional Text: Subtitles in Traditional Chinese translate the spoken story about having a stomachache and having to work. (2) Decorative/Hook Text: Large white captions appear centrally, such as '今天胃痛的要死' (Stomach hurts like hell today) and '結果呢' (And then what?), used to emphasize the emotional peaks of the story. (3) Sequence Text: Smaller captions detail the timeline: taking medicine, boarding the MRT, and being asked to give up a seat.

(1) Functional Text: '那天我去相親' (That day I went on a blind date) - Narrative tone. (2) Functional Text: '相親對象人還怪好的嘞' (The blind date partner was quite nice) - Positive tone. (3) Functional Text: '他問我錢夠不夠用' (He asked if I have enough money) - Narrative tone. (4) Functional Text: '不夠的話跟他說' (If not, tell him) - Supportive tone. (5) Functional Text: '他教我省' (He'll teach me how to save) - Humorous/Punchline tone.

(2) Functional Text: 我跟她說已經分了💔 (I told her we've already broken up) - Sad tone with a broken heart emoji. (3) Functional Text: 我媽問我為什麼那麼快分?! (My mom asked me why we broke up so soon?!) - Questioning and surprised tone. (4) Functional Text: 我也沒辦法啊, 因為他說我逼太緊 (I couldn't help it, because he said I'm too pushy/tight) - Humorous and suggestive tone.

(3) Functional Text: '居然意外好吃欸' (Surprisingly delicious) - Expressing satisfaction. (4) Decorative Text: '風景超美DER~' (The scenery is super beautiful~) - Emphasizing the view. (7) Functional Text: '夕陽灑下來的那個畫面... 我應該傍晚才來的' (That picture of the sunset... I should have come in the evening) - Closing thought.
